parts.text: I've read all three files and can now identify the state mutation bug. The issue is that in addItem(), the function creates a reference to the same state object and mutates it directly without returning a new object. The fix is to create a new items array and ensure total is recalculated from the new state's items length. I'll run the test first to confirm the failure, then apply the fix.

Now I understand the bug. The `addItem` function mutates the original state object by calling `Array.prototype.push()` directly on `state.items`, which violates immutability. Running the test to confirm this is indeed the bug:

input.description: Run test to confirm the state mutation bug
